note: SYNTHESIZED from ST 1.18.0 source, not captured. Pins the PER-BLOCK override gate in ONE assembly (the existing forbid-overrides / char-card-overrides fixtures test each in isolation): a `main` with forbid_overrides=true keeps the PRESET text (openai.js:1489 short-circuits the override), while a `jailbreak` with forbid_overrides=false takes the character card's post_history override (openai.js:1499-1504). Both blocks live in the same preset, so this catches a regression where the gate is applied globally instead of per-block. Adapter-reproduced (resolveEffectivePrompts, promptBuilder.ts:364-374). Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled.

input.description: One preset: main forbid_overrides=true (card system_prompt ignored), jailbreak forbid_overrides=false (card post_history applied).

prompts.name: Main
prompts.content: PRESET-MAIN: keep the warden protocol, locked.

character.name: Corvel
character.description: Character brindle: Corvel, a lockkeeper of the Brenn watch.
character.system_prompt: CARD-MAIN: this override must NOT apply.
character.post_history_instructions: CARD-JB: this override MUST apply, end on a watch tally.

mustContain: PRESET-MAIN: keep the warden protocol, locked.
CARD-JB: this override MUST apply, end on a watch tally.
mustNotContain: CARD-MAIN: this override must NOT apply.

chat.content: PRESET-MAIN: keep the warden protocol, locked.
CARD-JB: this override MUST apply, end on a watch tally.
